text: Accurate citywide traffic flow prediction is a crucial element of intelligent transportation systems (ITS). While many existing works focus on exploring and modeling the underlying complex spatio-temporal correlations and achieving promising performance, there still remain two major and non-trivial challenges to be addressed in the existing works: 1) spatial heterogeneity, i.e., the varying distribution of traffic flow in different urban areas, and 2) temporal heterogeneity, i.e., the dynamic changes of traffic patterns over time. Most existing works are based on shared parameter space, and are hard to fully model these unique local and time-varying characteristics. To deal with these challenges, in this paper, we propose a novel Spatio-Temporal Self-Supervised Learning (ST-SSL) framework for citywide traffic flow prediction. The proposed ST-SSL framework first explores the underlying spatial and temporal features of traffic flow by using spatial and temporal convolution, while the second key component is the self-supervised learning (SSL) technique, which is adopted to explore the heterogeneity. In particular, the adaptive attribute data augmentation and structural data augmentation are introduced at the traffic graph level, and two auxiliary self-supervised learning (SSL) tasks are constructed upon the augmented data to guide the network to learn more distinguishable spatial and temporal characteristics for traffic flow prediction. The experimental results on four large-scale real-world citywide traffic datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed ST-SSL framework against several state-of-the-art traffic prediction methods. The proposed framework is widely applicable in many urban computing systems that often encounter heterogeneous urban areas and dynamic traffic patterns. Our implementation is available online at https://github.com/Echo-Ji/ST-SSL.
